thought.text: Let me look at several different test cases to discover all needed tokens:

reasoning_content: I'm looking at preprocessed C code where I need to parse the `# linenum filename flags` directives at the start to track the actual source file and line numbers. The key is updating the location information in the output while also handling all the C tokens the tests require, plus tracking which tokens start a physical line and which have leading whitespace.

action.command: cd /workspace/YatCC && for f in test/cases/functional-3/056_short_circuit.sysu.c test/cases/functional-3/064_while_if.sysu.c test/cases/functional-2/026_arr_defn.sysu.c test/cases/functional-1/011_add.sysu.c; do echo "=== $f ==="; cat "$f"; done

tool_call.name: terminal
tool_call.arguments: {"command": "cd /workspace/YatCC && for f in test/cases/functional-3/056_short_circuit.sysu.c test/cases/functional-3/064_while_if.sysu.c test/cases/functional-2/026_arr_defn.sysu.c test/cases/functional-1/011_add.sysu.c; do echo \"=== $f ===\"; cat \"$f\"; done", "security_risk": "LOW", "summary": "Inspect representative test sources"}
